Patch Set 10:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/634501ba-95b8-11e2-bc9e-52540035b04c).  Ran 20 tests.  5 tests failed: sanity-quota, replay-ost-single, conf-sanity, replay-single, sanity.

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(11 inline comments)

Liang, please comment on the oddity in lnet selftest code?

Sebastien: I think it's really overkill to add LASSERTs for values not being NULL and such when in fact they are dereferenced right away and so the system will crash either way. those LASSERTs are not free too, both in terms of CPU cost and stack cost.
And some of them downright could never happen.
I know coverity probably thinks otherwise, but I hope there is a way to tell it to ignore certain places.

parents.subject: LU-3807 dcache: Unsafe error handling arnd ll_splice_alias

commit.subject: LU-2217 build: fix 'NULL pointer dereference' errors
commit.message:
LU-2217 build: fix 'NULL pointer dereference' errors

Fix 'NULL pointer dereference' defects found by Coverity version
6.5.3:
Dereference after null check (FORWARD_NULL)
For instance, Passing null pointer to a function which dereferences
it.
Dereference before null check (REVERSE_INULL)
Null-checking variable suggests that it may be null, but it has
already been dereferenced on all paths leading to the check.
Dereference null return value (NULL_RETURNS)

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sebastien.buisson@bull.net>
Change-Id: I646d75d488b66ed348ec51cc2b69f0b644474c30
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/4720
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
